Trigger: timber_log node depleted
Notes: Wood drop.

Trigger: wild_herbs node depleted
Notes: Herbs feed potions and cooking — both live in chemistry.

Trigger: iron_ore node depleted
permafrost_ore node depleted
Notes: Tundra-only — highest gathering yield.

Trigger: copper_deposit node depleted
Notes: Desert-only.

Trigger: flint_outcrop node depleted
Notes: Cheap baseline.

Trigger: frozen_pond node depleted
Notes: Fish are also cooking input.

Trigger: any unhandled resource node
Notes: Default fallback.

Trigger: wildlife enemy death credited to player
Notes: Hunting > Animals branch flavor. baseHuntXp scales with creature tier; survival side-grant always positive.

Trigger: villain/raider death credited to player
Notes: Same routing as animals; doubled if target is on an active bounty contract (see below).

Trigger: bounty contract target killed/captured
Notes: Animal contracts unlocked by hunting.bounty.1. contractBaseXp scales with contract rarity.

Trigger: villain NPC contract completed
Notes: Villain contracts unlocked by hunting.bounty.4. 1.5× base XP and gold reward.

Trigger: enemy death + equipped weapon class = pistol
Notes: Combat XP gated on weapon class. Hunting still side-grants.

Trigger: enemy death + equipped weapon class = rifle
enemy death + equipped weapon class = blade
Notes: Sword/dagger/scythe.

Trigger: enemy death + equipped weapon class = hammer
Notes: Hammer/axe/blunt.

Trigger: successful pickpocket attempt (Theft 1+ required)
Notes: Unlocked by combat.theft.1.

Trigger: lock opened with picks
Notes: Speed bonus from combat.theft.2.

Trigger: stealth kill from behind (Theft 4+ required)
Notes: 1.5× multiplier; counts as kill for hunting side-grant.

Trigger: recipe id starts with 'build_'
Notes: Any building schematic.

Trigger: cook_* recipe completes
Notes: Currently routes cook_meat/cook_fish to chemistry — keep all cook_* on chemistry. boil_water/craft_bandage = medicinal (also chemistry).

Trigger: brew_* recipe completes
Notes: All brew_* recipes route to chemistry alongside cook_*.

Trigger: any other crafting recipe completes
Notes: Weapons / armour / tools / attachments.

Trigger: hire_* completes via successful dialog and the NPC settles into a tent in your claimed camp
Notes: NO GOLD COST. Recruitment requires: (1) dialog success per the Recruit Method in npc-hires.csv, (2) a placed claim_flag, (3) campfire + storage crate in camp, (4) one personal tent per NPC. First hire of each role grants +50% (×22 instead of 15).

Trigger: charisma/resolve/combat/survival check passes during recruit dialog
Notes: Granted whether or not the NPC actually joins (they may decline for other reasons). Side-grants 1 XP to whichever profession governed the check.

Activity: Place claim flag (first time per area)
Trigger: claim_flag placed in an unclaimed map sector
Notes: Stakes the area for recruitment. Subsequent flags in the same sector grant 0 XP (no farming). Also broadcasts your guild logo to nearby NPCs and to the world map. Will likely trigger a raid timer within 1-3 in-game days.

Trigger: raid resolved with claim_flag still standing
Notes: Per raid (not per ally — that's the separate Defend ally row). Caps at 1 grant per raid.

Trigger: faction pledge confirmed at Embassy
Notes: One-time grant per faction. Pledging unlocks faction contracts and turns the other four hostile.

Trigger: reputation crosses an upward threshold (Neutral→Friendly→Honored→Allied→Hero)
Notes: Grants once per tier per faction. See faction-reputation-tiers.csv for the full 8-tier scale. Maximum 4 positive tier-ups per faction = 100 XP/faction.

Trigger: reputation climbs back across a NEGATIVE threshold (Hated→Hostile→Unfriendly→Neutral)
Notes: Reward for digging out of a hostile state. Granted once per recovery; if you tank back into negative the second crossing grants nothing.

Trigger: faction-specific quest accepted at Embassy completes
Notes: Each faction has signature professions per factions.csv (e.g. Keepers route to combat or crafting/armour; Scavengers to crafting/gunsmith). Side-grant always township. Contracts only available at Friendly+ rep.

Activity: Defeat hostile-faction NPC in their territory
Trigger: kill an NPC of a Hostile or Hated faction inside their declared territory
Notes: Self-defense XP for the inevitable result of pledging — the two natural-enemy factions go Hostile and you'll fight them in their lands. Tagged so we don't double-count with the standard combat-kill XP.

Trigger: guild charter signed at Meeting Hall
Notes: Requires Diplomacy 2 + Meeting Hall built. Guild members share rep gains and a banner.

Trigger: 5+ ally NPCs settled in camp radius
Notes: Tribe unlocks shared cooking pot (passive food) and morale aura.

Trigger: 10+ ally NPCs settled in camp radius
Notes: Village unlocks Trade Post payouts and tier-2 building schematics.

Trigger: 20+ ally NPCs settled in camp radius
Notes: Town unlocks Bazaar slots tier-3 buildings and the Town Banner (faction reputation broadcast).

Trigger: allied NPC survives a raid in camp radius
Notes: Per ally per raid. Caps at 10 allies/raid to prevent farming.

Trigger: envoy returns from faction with positive outcome
Notes: Diplomat Envoy hire required; mission cooldown 1 in-game day.

Trigger: any NPC (ally or enemy) triggers a player-equivalent XP event
Notes: NPCs run the SAME XP table as the player at half rate by default. A Woodcutter chopping a tree gains gathering XP. A raider killing a hire gains combat XP. Each NPC tracks its own profession ranks and unlocks perks the same way the player does — visible on the NPC inspect panel.

Trigger: NPC crosses a profession rank threshold
Notes: Mirrors the player rank-up flow. NPC chooses a perk from the same tree the player would; allied NPCs use the perk in support of you raiders use it against you.

Trigger: player wields the Signature Item listed in professions.csv
Notes: Wielding a profession's signature item grants +25% XP for that profession's gain events. Currently scoped to Combat (per-branch weapon class) and Hunting (Bow/Sniper/Skinning Knife/Fishing Rod/Spyglass/Snare Kit). Other professions list signatures (Hatchet/Pickaxe/Ice Pickaxe/Resonator/Scrap Cutter/Hammer/Trowel/Coinpurse/Scroll/Turret Schematic/Pan/Pestle/Detonator/Vial/Tongs/Training Sword/Cloak/Pack/Medal) for flavor only.
